method: Each golden_event_id was chosen by reading a specific corpus event, then authoring a plain-language question that event answers, under an anti-leakage rubric (no verbatim distinctive tokens; answerable from the enriched node, not raw text; one unique golden per question; no aggregation/superlative/temporal questions). Authored + adversarially leak-verified + repaired per source, then globally de-duplicated and validated via `hippo-bench qa validate --min-scoreable 100`. Claude source now reads agentic_sessions (harness=claude-code) per the schema-v18 agentic unification; goldens are claude-{agentic_sessions.id}.
IMPORTANT: golden_event_ids are bound to this exact corpus_content_hash. If corpus-v2 is rebuilt from a different live DB, the sampled events change and these goldens will no longer resolve. Re-run `hippo-bench qa validate` after any corpus rebuild; if it fails, re-annotate against the new corpus (see QA-ANNOTATION.md).
follow_up: qa-071/qa-008/qa-034 share several topical (non-identifier) tokens with their golden's title/command; acceptable under the rubric but could be paraphrased further in a future polish pass. Growing to >=150 scoreable items for fine-grained model ranking is tracked separately (trust-tracking P2-03 / issue #133).
